text:
There was a girl named Bella who loved to read. She’d carry books everywhere with her, hidden behind clothes, tucked into notebooks. Her favorite was ‘Anne of Green Gables’.

One evening, after closing up the library, Bella found an old wooden box with no label beneath a coat rack. Inside was a dusty tome. Flipping it open, she read of adventures long forgotten and mysterious secrets kept in the quiet town she lived in. It spoke of a young girl with big dreams who lived on pretending.

Bella spent weeks lost in those pages. She imagined herself as Anne, navigating a world where she was neither orphan nor fool and embarking on fantastical escapades. She saw herself helping lions and befriending unicorns.

One winter night, a gust lifted a stray sock from Bella’s bed. Inside, she found the book. It felt more real than paper and ink could be.

The next morning, she put on her coat, threw on a hat, grabbed her book
